mission.title: oauth2-proxy: New Feature: Support Oauth2 token introspection as per rfc7662#section2.1
mission.description: New Feature: Support Oauth2 token introspection as per rfc7662#section2.1. Requested by 12+ users.

steps.title: Check current oauth2-proxy deployment
steps.description:
Verify your oauth2-proxy version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n oauth2-proxy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=oauth2-proxy
helm list -n oauth2-proxy 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working oauth2-proxy installation.

steps.title: Review oauth2-proxy configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant oauth2-proxy configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n oauth2-proxy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=oauth2-proxy
kubectl get configmap -n oauth2-proxy -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=oauth2-proxy
```
## Expected Behavior

When calling the endpoint with directly an opaque (or not) access token, Oauth2Proxy should be able to validate the token.

steps.title: Apply the fix for New Feature: Support Oauth2 token introspection as per…
steps.description:
This PR add feature of Token Introspection based on [RFC7662 OAuth 2.0 Token Introspection](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7662.html).
This PR add feature to validate bearer token (access token) passed by authori
```yaml
--provider: oidc # or any other
      --skip-bearer-tokens: true
      --token-instropection-url: https://IDPROVIDERSERVICE.com/api/auth/token/info
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n oauth2-proxy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=oauth2-proxy
kubectl get events -n oauth2-proxy --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "New Feature: Support Oauth2 token introspection as per…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
This PR add feature of Token Introspection based on [RFC7662 OAuth 2.0 Token Introspection](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7662.html).
This PR add feature to validate bearer token (access token) passed by authori

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with oauth2-proxy installed or the issue environment reproducible.
